title: pelargonium × hammersleiae
intro: Pelargonium × hammersleiae, also known as Geranium or Cape Rose, is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of geraniol and linalool. The health benefits of Pelargonium × hammersleiae include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or extracting the essential oil from the flowers.
